response.justification: Wei Chen is a strong hands-on senior/staff engineer with nearly 20 years of experience, deep software craftsmanship, and genuine open-source contributions, but the profile falls well short of a director-level principal role centered on AI strategy. AI exposure is recent and tooling-focused (MCP generators, agent infrastructure) rather than recognized expertise with demonstrated AI products or platforms, and there is no evidence of the strategic advisory, organizational influence, or executive-level engineering leadership this role requires.
strengths: Nearly 20 years of broad engineering experience across web, embedded, low-latency, and cloud systems
Founder experience (GISCollective) showing entrepreneurial initiative and long-term product ownership
Active knowledge-sharing via conference talks, workshops, and open-source libraries
concerns: No demonstrated director-level scope or engineering leadership; career has stayed at senior/staff IC delivery level
AI experience is recent and infrastructural, not recognized expertise or AI-powered products at scale
No advanced degree in an AI-related field and limited cloud-platform strategy background

key_factors.factor: Lack of AI strategy and recognized AI expertise
Seniority gap vs. director-level principal scope
Long, broad engineering track record with founder experience
